incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS)
incidents.vector: Data-wiper malware deployed via compromised Microsoft endpoint management (Intune), erasing data from over 200,000 systems, servers and mobile devices across 79 countries
incidents.impact: Global operations shut down; 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland alone. Devices wiped and defaced with attacker branding. CISA issued advisory urging endpoint management hardening. Attack claimed as retaliation for Feb 28 missile strike on Iranian school.

incidents.vector: Self-propagating worm (CanisterWorm) targeting exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers, deploying wiper payload triggered by Iran timezone or Farsi locale
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on all Kubernetes cluster nodes or local machines matching Iranian locale. TeamPCP group injecting itself into Iran conflict to cover financially motivated operations.

incidents.vector: Drone activity disrupting Amazon Web Services' Bahrain data center region amid Middle East conflict
incidents.impact: Second disruption in a month to AWS operations in the region. Cloud services for customers relying on the Bahrain region affected.

incidents.vector: Compromised private key used to bypass minting controls and illicitly create $80 million in uncollateralized USR stablecoin
incidents.impact: Attacker converted illicit tokens to approximately $24.5 million in ETH. USR stablecoin depegged from $1 to $0.26. Platform paused operations and is attempting recovery.

incidents.vector: Phishing campaign targeting Signal messaging app accounts of current and former U.S. government officials, military personnel, political figures and journalists via fake automated support notices
incidents.impact: Thousands of accounts compromised globally. Attackers gained access to messages, contact lists, and used compromised accounts for further phishing. FBI and CISA issued joint advisory.

incidents.vector: AI-generated phishing lures delivered via Railway PaaS infrastructure, exploiting Microsoft device authentication flow to steal OAuth tokens valid for 90 days without needing passwords or MFA
incidents.impact: Hundreds of organizations compromised across construction, law, nonprofits, real estate, manufacturing, finance, healthcare and government sectors. Pace accelerated dramatically since early March.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of multiple zero-day and known vulnerabilities in Cisco SD-WAN and firewall management software (9 CVEs total), with Interlock ransomware exploiting CVE-2026-20131 since January
incidents.impact: Five of nine vulnerabilities confirmed actively exploited. Interlock ransomware group had zero-day access for over a month before disclosure. Targets include education, healthcare, manufacturing and government.

incidents.vector: Supply chain attack injecting credential-stealing malware into official Trivy GitHub Actions releases
incidents.impact: Malicious versions harvested SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens and cryptocurrency wallets from CI/CD pipelines before Aqua Security removed the compromised files.

incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS)
incidents.vector: Custom malware disguised as AI tools (Pictory), password managers (KeePass), and Telegram, using Telegram bots as command-and-control infrastructure for surveillance
incidents.impact: Screen and audio recordings, file exfiltration, and monitoring of targeted Iranian dissidents and journalists. Death threats and bounties sent to victims. DOJ seized four MOIS-linked domains.

incidents.vector: Systematic diversion of U.S.-manufactured AI servers with controlled GPUs to China via Southeast Asian shell company, using falsified documents and staged dummy servers to deceive compliance audits
incidents.impact: Approximately $2.5 billion worth of export-controlled AI servers diverted to China between 2024-2025. Three individuals indicted; two arrested. Scheme undermined U.S. national security export controls.

incidents.attacker: China — state-suspected espionage group; Iran — state-linked cyber actors
incidents.vector: EU imposed cyber sanctions on entities in China and Iran for conducting cyberattacks against EU member states and allies
incidents.impact: Sanctions target companies and individuals involved in sustained cyber espionage campaigns. Signals escalating EU response to state-sponsored cyber threats.

incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised routers and web cameras to launch hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks, including against Department of Defense infrastructure
incidents.impact: Over 300,000 combined attack commands launched. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. DOJ dismantled infrastructure with international law enforcement cooperation.

incidents.victim: Residential and small business router owners (369,000 IPs)
incidents.vector: SocksEscort malware infected home and small business routers, selling proxy access to cybercriminals who used it to mask identities for bank account takeovers and cryptocurrency theft
incidents.impact: Enabled millions of dollars in fraud including $1M cryptocurrency theft, $700K business fraud, and $100K in military member fraud. International law enforcement dismantled the network across multiple countries.

incidents.vector: Initial access broker exploited vulnerabilities in corporate networks and sold access to Yanluowang ransomware group and other cybercrime operations
incidents.impact: Russian citizen Aleksei Volkov sentenced to 81 months in prison. Facilitated dozens of ransomware attacks causing $9M+ in actual losses and $24M+ in intended losses across U.S. organizations.

incidents.victim: Ubiquiti UniFi Network Application users (88,000 exposed)
incidents.vector: Maximum-severity path-traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-22557, CVSS 10) in UniFi Network Application enabling unauthenticated remote account takeover
incidents.impact: Nearly 88,000 hosts publicly exposed. Exploitation requires no authentication and is trivially automatable. Patches released but version detection not possible via scanning.

incidents.vector: Maximum-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-29000, CVSS 10) in pac4j Java security engine allows authentication bypass via forged JWT tokens using only a server's public RSA key
incidents.impact: Attacker can gain highest privileges on affected systems. Library integrated into Spring Security, Play Framework, Vert.x, Javalin and others. PoC exploit is public; no confirmed exploitation yet.
